other_observers: Mike and Pat Piborn (Homeowners) Kay McConnell, Dick Filby; Piburns found this bird
specific_location: Home of Mike and Pat Piburn, 158 S. Bill Creek Rd. Carbondale, CO (Next to BRB Campground on Crystal River)
description: DESCRIPTION – The decurved bill and brown/gray coloring immediately made this bird a Curve-billed Thrasher. Size was about the same as a Robin. The decurved bill was all dark and over an inch long and the iris was yellow. Coloring was uniform on the head and wings and lighter mottled brown/gray on the breast and belly Also the throat showed faint malar streaking. I did not notice wing-bars while observing the bird, but looking at the photos one can see very faint wing-bars. The lack of strongish wing-bars puts this into the Arizona ssp. (palmeri) rather than with the birds that are resident SE Colorado (oberholseri). Other observers who saw the bird(s) with you: Kay McConnell, Mike and Pat Piburn, Dick FilbyBehaviors: Flew in from ??? and moved around through the oak scrub occasionally in the open. The bird was fairly secretive.Call: Viewed through window/did not vocalizePlumage: Adult Basic
photographer: Photographer: Pat Pihorn, Pit Pihorn, Pat Pihorn, Pat Pihorn, Pat Pihorn
